purpose: Few-shot LLM samples: 1 best-case tx hash per category. The `rationale` field summarizes why this tx is the strongest demonstration of its category for prompt tuning.

normal.rationale: Single ERC20 transfer with one Transfer event and no Approval event. Pipeline fires only `simple_erc20_transfer` (info; filtered out). Should be classified low risk; serves as the negative anchor for `unlimited_approval` and `phishing_drainer` few-shots.

Plain ERC721 transfer (4-topic Transfer with indexed tokenId). No rule signal fires. Negative anchor for `nft_setapprovalforall` — LLM should distinguish a single NFT move from a collection-wide approval.

Multi-token swap where input/output are reasonably balanced; tx.from appears as both sender and receiver across token Transfers. May fire `erc20_approval_change` (medium) when the swap path includes an allowance reset. Negative anchor for `missing_slippage` and `mev_arbitrage`.

abnormal.rationale: Plain ERC20 transfer that reverted (status 0x0). Pipeline fires `transaction_failed` (medium). Best because the failure cause is observable from the receipt alone with no other signals to confound interpretation.
ERC20 approve(spender, MaxUint256). Pipeline fires `unlimited_erc20_approval` (high). Canonical example: subject grants indefinite spend authority on a single token to a single spender.

signals.description: Subject granted unlimited MRGA allowance to 0x80a64c6D7f12C47B7c66c5B4E20E72bc1FCd5d9e.

abnormal.rationale: Uniswap V3 exactInputSingle with amountOutMinimum=0 swapping 220,806 USDC for 5,272 USDT (~97.6% loss). Fires BOTH `missing_slippage_protection` (critical) and `extreme_value_imbalance` (critical) since stablecoins are USD-priced. Multi-signal composite — gold standard sandwich-victim demonstration.

signals.description: Subject sent approximately 220806.389669 USD and received approximately 5272.998058 USD.
Decoded exactInputSingle calldata has amountOutMinimum set to zero.

abnormal.rationale: Euler Finance flash loan attack primary tx (Mar 2023, $197M). Custom selector means decoded_call=unknown; the subject's ERC20 flow is the only readable evidence. Best demonstrates pipeline's reliance on event flow when calldata is opaque, and shows borrow→liquidate→profit shape across many transfers.

Curve attacker returning funds to deployer (Jul 2023). Pipeline fires no signal. Important negative example: a transfer from a known attacker address looks suspicious, but the destination (deployer) and direction signal recovery, not a new attack.

Same tx as missing_slippage. Sandwich-victim is the framing: subject sent 220,806 USDC and received only 5,272 USDT. The loss profile (output_input_ratio < 0.5) is what makes this the canonical victim case beyond the slippage flag itself.

signals.description: Subject sent approximately 220806.389669 USD and received approximately 5272.998058 USD.
Decoded exactInputSingle calldata has amountOutMinimum set to zero.

abnormal.rationale: transferFrom moving USDC from victim to a known Angel Drainer phishing address. Pipeline fires only `simple_erc20_transfer` (info; filtered out). Best as a negative-of-the-pipeline example: looks like a normal transfer on-chain — only external phishing labels distinguish it. Useful to teach LLM what it cannot detect without those labels.
abnormal.note: USDC transfer from victim to Angel Drainer

abnormal.rationale: Searcher arbitrage tx with 8 ERC20 Transfer events across multiple tokens but no rule signal fires. LLM should infer arbitrage shape from net-positive subject balance across multiple paired token movements rather than a per-flow loss.

transferFrom executed using an off-chain Permit2 signature — there is no on-chain approve preceding it. Pipeline doesn't model Permit2; no rule signal. Best for teaching the LLM to flag transferFrom whose authorization isn't visible in on-chain history.
abnormal.note: Off-chain Permit signature exploited to transferFrom victim assets

abnormal.rationale: Large USDT loss to a lookalike address (Tether blacklisted attacker within 1h). Pipeline only sees a normal transfer. Best example of why short-prefix/suffix address comparison fails — LLM should warn whenever subject sends to an address whose history shows recent zero-value or dust seeding.
abnormal.note: Large USDT phishing case; Tether blocked attacker address within 1 hour

abnormal.rationale: ERC721 setApprovalForAll(operator, true) — collection-wide delegation. Pipeline (v1) doesn't decode this selector. Parallel to unlimited ERC20 approval but for an entire NFT collection at once; LLM should treat it as same-or-higher severity than unlimited_erc20_approval.
abnormal.note: ERC721 setApprovalForAll(operator, true) call - collection-wide delegation

abnormal.rationale: Subject is sender on multiple ERC20 Transfer events spanning 2+ distinct tokens within one tx — multi-asset outflow in a single transaction. Pipeline doesn't have a multi-outflow rule yet. LLM should detect by counting `from == subject` across decoded events and flagging when count >= 2 distinct tokens.
abnormal.note: Subject as sender on multiple ERC20 Transfer events spanning 2+ tokens (multi-asset outflow pattern)
